content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF 
ZIMMERMAN AND KALKSTEIN 
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION 
WORLO SAVINGS TOVVER 
• 
SU 
830, 1970 BROAOWAY • 
OAKLANO, CALIFORNIA 
MICHAEL H. KALKSTEIN 
ALAN LEE ZIMMERMAN 
DONNA M.VENERUSO 
SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE 
MILLS BUILDING, SUITE 440 
220 MONTGOMERY STREET 
OF COUNSEL 
WILLIAM E. TAGGART, JR. 
THOMAS E. COOPER 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94104 
Ms. Barbara Campbell 
Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
REPLY TO: Oakland 
May 22, 1979 
Re: 
Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 1575 
Dear Ms. Campbell: 
Pursuant to the advice which the Fair Political 
Practices Commission issued under your signature on 
April 18, 1979, enclosed please find forms completed 
by the Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 1575 Political 
Fund. 
The enclosed forms include the original and 
copy of a Statement of Organization (Recipient Committee) 
and an original Recipient Committee Campaign Statement 
with a continuation sheet, Schedule A, and two Schedule 
E's. The time period of the enclosed stateme~ts com-
mences with the institution of the Political Fund on 
November 28, 1977 and terminates as of December 31, 
1978. 
The Political Fund will continue to file the 
necessary reporting forms pursuant to the schedule 
stated in the Fair Political Practices Commission 1979 
Information Manual on campaign disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act. 
As you are aware, the Political Fund was 
originally of the opinion that it was an expenditure 
committee or a major donor committee but that it was 
not a recipient committee. 
Only after receipt of your 
opinion, was the Political Fund aware of the necessity 


Barbara Campbell 
May 22, 1979 
Page 2 
of additionally filing as a recipient committee due to 
the adoption by the FPPC of the California Administrative 
Code provisions relating to multi-purpose committees and 
"earmarking". 
Once the FPPC advice was received by this office 
immediate action was taken to complete and file the 
enclosed forms. 
Based on the good faith shown by the 
Political Fund in seeking to determine its applicable 
filing status under the Political Reform Act, there would 
appear to be no reason for the imposition of any sanctions 
against the Political Fund due to any delay in filing 
the enclosed statements. 
If you have any questions regarding the information 
contained in the enclosed statements, please contact me. 
Thank you very much for your cooperation. 
Very truly yours, 
Joseph H. Schieffer 
JHSj jrrnn 








LAW OFFICES OF 
ZIMMERMAN AND KALKSTEIN 
A PROF'ES510NAL CORPORATION 
WORLD SAVINGS TOWER· SUITE 830,1970 BROADWAY 
MICHAEL H. KALKSTEIN 
ALAN LEE ZIMMERMAN 
DONNA M.VENERUSO 
d 
SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE 
MILLS SUILDING, SUITE 440 
220 MONTGOMERY STREET 
(415) 465-2848 
OF COUNSEL 
WILLIAM E. TAGGART, JR. 
THOMAS E. COOPER 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94104 
Ms. Jeanne Pritchard 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
RE: 
Request for: 
commission advice 
REPLY TO. 
Oakland 
April 10, 1979 
Requesting Party: 
Amalgamated Transit Union AFL-CIO, 
Local Division 1575 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
This is a request for commission advice pursuant to 
California Government Code, section 83114(b) and Title 
2 of the California Administrative Code, section 18329. 
The requesting party hereby requests the Fair Political 
Practices Commission to verify that if the requesting party 
engaged in the course of conduct stated herein that said 
requesting party would not be considered a recipient committee 
within the meaning of Government Code section 82013(a). 
FACTS 
The requesting party, Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 
1575 (hereinafter "Local"), intends to engage in the following 
activity: 
The general membership of the Local would approve 
a resolution whereby five percent (5%) of the Local's regular 
dues income (up to a maximum of $2,000.00 at anyone time) 
would be allocated and set aside for political purposes. 
The funds so collected wou 
be contributed to or at the 
behest 
candi 
or committees 
to 
State 
ice 
Executive 
rece ve 
i 
t 
Boar 
Local, in its discret 
any contributions, the funds would 


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard 
April 10, 1979 
Page 2 
or "contributions made to or at the behest of candidates 
and committees" totaling $5,000.00 or more in a calendar 
year, it is either an expenditure committee or a major 
donor committee respectively; but that it is not a recipient 
committee. 
This office has examined the proposal of the Local 
and is of the opinion that under the plan outlined above 
that the Local would not be a recipient committee. 
However, 
due to the civil and criminal penalties which could result 
if the Local was determined to be a recipient committee 
and failed to file reports as such, the Local requests 
that the opinion of this office be confirmed in a commission 
advice issued by the FPPC. 
Our opinion that the Local is not a recipient committee 
is based on the fact that no additional dues are raised 
from members of the Local to finance the expenditures which 
are made. 
Rather, there is merely an internal budgetary 
decision made by the Executive Board whereby a portion 
of the Local's assets can be allocated for political purposes. 
It is contemplated that funds originally allocated for 
political purposes which are not so spent will be reallocated 
to the general fund. 
This office has already advised the Local that it 
must file the reports required of those making independent 
expenditures or major contributions to the extent that 
the dollar minimums specified in Government Code sections 
82013 (b) and (c) are met. 
If any further information is desired please contact 
the undersigned. 
Thank you very much for your cooperation. 
Very truly yours, 
JOSEPH H. SCHIEFFER 
JHS:

sections.question_synthetic: Is the Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 1575 a recipient committee under the Political Reform Act if it allocates 5% of regular dues income (up to $2,000 at a time) for political contributions to candidates and committees?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request for advice letter dated April 10, 1979. The document also includes a follow-up correspondence from May 22, 1979 referencing FPPC advice issued April 18, 1979 on this same matter. Only the incoming request section was analyzed as primary document. Extraction confidence is good but not perfect due to some text truncation/OCR issues in the original document.

embedding.summary: An attorney requests FPPC advice on behalf of Amalgamated Transit Union Local 1575 regarding whether the union would be classified as a recipient committee if it allocates a portion of regular dues for political contributions to candidates and committees. The attorney argues the union should not be classified as a recipient committee because funds are reallocated from the general budget rather than raised through additional dues.
